 {"id":"git_safety_guard-69d7","title":"[E7-T7] Comprehensive testing for Interactive Learning Mode","description":"## Comprehensive Unit & E2E Testing for Epic 7: Interactive Learning Mode

### Overview
Testing for the interactive mode where users can press a key to add blocked commands to allowlist with security safeguards.

### Unit Tests Required

#### 1. TTY Detection Tests (tests/unit/test_tty_detection.rs)
```rust
#[cfg(test)]
mod tests {
    use std::io::IsTerminal;
    
    #[test]
    fn test_detects_interactive_terminal() {
        // Mock terminal detection
        let result = is_interactive_session();
        // In test environment, typically not a TTY
        assert!(!result); // Verify it correctly detects non-TTY
    }
    
    #[test]
    fn test_ci_environment_detection() {
        // CI environment should never be interactive
        std::env::set_var("CI", "true");
        assert!(!is_interactive_session());
        std::env::remove_var("CI");
    }
    
    #[test]
    fn test_dumb_terminal_detection() {
        std::env::set_var("TERM", "dumb");
        assert!(!is_interactive_session());
        std::env::remove_var("TERM");
    }
    
    #[test]
    fn test_interactive_mode_disabled_in_pipe() {
        // When stdout is piped, should not be interactive
        // This is architecture for testing, not a direct unit test
    }
}
```

#### 2. Security Safeguard Tests
```rust
#[test]
fn test_learning_mode_requires_explicit_opt_in() {
    // Learning mode should be OFF by default
    let config = Config::default();
    assert!(!config.learning_mode_enabled);
}

#[test]
fn test_critical_severity_cannot_be_learned() {
    let result = can_learn_pattern("rm -rf /", Severity::Critical);
    assert!(!result);
}

#[test]
fn test_learning_mode_disabled_on_protected_branches() {
    // On main/master, learning should be disabled
    let result = can_learn_in_context(Context { branch: "main" });
    assert!(!result);
}

#[test]
fn test_confirmation_required_for_high_severity() {
    // P1/P2 patterns should require explicit confirmation
    let result = requires_confirmation(Severity::High);
    assert!(result);
}
```

#### 3. Allowlist Entry Generation Tests
- test_learned_entry_includes_context
- test_learned_entry_is_scoped_to_directory
- test_learned_entry_has_expiration_option
- test_generated_entry_syntax_is_valid

### Integration Tests (tests/integration/interactive_learning.rs)

```rust
#[test]
fn test_learning_prompt_appears_in_terminal() {
    // Use PTY to simulate terminal
    let pty = spawn_dcg_in_pty(&["check", "blocked-command"]);
    let output = pty.read_until_prompt();
    assert!(output.contains("Add to allowlist?") || output.contains("[a]llow"));
}

#[test]
fn test_learning_does_not_appear_in_ci() {
    std::env::set_var("CI", "true");
    let output = run_dcg_check("blocked-command");
    assert!(!output.contains("Add to allowlist?"));
    std::env::remove_var("CI");
}

#[test]
fn test_keypress_adds_to_allowlist() {
    let pty = spawn_dcg_in_pty(&["check", "npm run custom"]);
    pty.send_key('a'); // Press 'a' to allow
    
    // Verify entry was added
    let config = read_config();
    assert!(config.allowlist.contains("npm run custom"));
}
```

### E2E Test Scenarios (tests/e2e/interactive/)

#### Scenario 1: Interactive Learning E2E (test_interactive.sh)
```bash
#!/bin/bash
LOG_FILE="${TEST_LOG_DIR}/interactive_$(date +%Y%m%d_%H%M%S).log"
log() { echo "[$(date '+%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S')] $*" | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"; }

log "=== E2E Test: Interactive Learning Mode ==="

# This test requires PTY simulation - use expect or similar
if ! command -v expect &> /dev/null; then
    log "SKIP: expect not installed, cannot test interactive mode"
    exit 0
fi

TEMP_DIR=$(mktemp -d)
export DCG_CONFIG="$TEMP_DIR/.dcg.toml"

# Enable learning mode
cat > "$DCG_CONFIG" << 'EOF'
[settings]
learning_mode = true
EOF

log "Step 1: Run dcg check in interactive mode"
expect << 'EXPECT_SCRIPT'
spawn dcg check "npm run custom-script"
expect {
    "Add to allowlist" {
        send "a"
        exp_continue
    }
    "Added to allowlist" {
        exit 0
    }
    timeout {
        exit 1
    }
}
EXPECT_SCRIPT

if [ $? -eq 0 ]; then
    log "PASS: Interactive learning completed"
else
    log "FAIL: Interactive learning did not complete"
    exit 1
fi

log "Step 2: Verify entry in config"
if grep -q "npm run custom-script" "$DCG_CONFIG"; then
    log "PASS: Entry added to config"
else
    log "FAIL: Entry not found in config"
    exit 1
fi

rm -rf "$TEMP_DIR"
log "=== Interactive learning test passed ==="
```

#### Scenario 2: Security Safeguard Testing
- Test that critical commands show warning even with learning mode
- Test that protected branch detection works
- Test rate limiting on learning operations

#### Scenario 3: Non-Interactive Fallback
- Test graceful fallback when not in TTY
- Test CI environment detection
- Test piped input/output handling

### Test Fixtures
- tests/fixtures/interactive_config.toml - Config with learning mode
- tests/fixtures/expect_scripts/ - Expect scripts for PTY testing

### Logging Requirements
- Log TTY detection results
- Log security check results (why learning was allowed/blocked)
- Log keypress events and responses
- Log config file modifications

### Success Criteria
- [ ] TTY detection works correctly
- [ ] CI environments never get interactive prompts
- [ ] Critical severity patterns cannot be learned
- [ ] Protected branch detection works
- [ ] Learning creates correctly formatted allowlist entries
- [ ] Graceful degradation when not interactive

### Dependencies

- Depends on: E7-T1 through E7-T6 (implementation)","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-18T19:51:54.832531393Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-18T20:37:53.044239612Z","compaction_level":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-69d7","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-qnci","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-18T19:55:23.211175298Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-69d7","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-v2ti","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-18T19:54:07.118557244Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","metadata":"","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"git_safety_guard-6ao","title":"Feature: Codecov Cloud Integration for Coverage Trend Tracking","description":"# Feature: Codecov Cloud Integration

## Background

DCG currently generates coverage reports using `cargo-llvm-cov` and stores them as CI artifacts. While this provides point-in-time coverage data, it lacks:

1. **Historical trend tracking** - Can't see if coverage is improving or declining over time
2. **PR coverage diffs** - No automatic comments showing what lines a PR adds/removes from coverage
3. **Coverage badges** - No README badge showing current coverage status
4. **Cross-branch comparison** - Can't compare feature branch coverage against main

CASS (coding_agent_session_search) has Codecov integration that provides all of these. This feature brings DCG to parity.

## Why This Matters for DCG

DCG enforces strict coverage thresholds:
- 70% overall minimum
- 80% for src/evaluator.rs (core decision logic)
- 80% for src/hook.rs (output formatting)

These thresholds are enforced but not tracked over time. With Codecov:
- We can see coverage trends (are we improving?)
- PRs automatically show coverage impact
- New code that drops coverage is immediately visible
- Team can set coverage targets and track progress

## Technical Approach

### Codecov Setup
1. Codecov is free for open source repositories
2. No token required for public repos (DCG is public)
3. Uses GitHub App integration for PR comments

### CI Changes
Modify `.github/workflows/ci.yml` coverage job:

```yaml
- name: Upload to Codecov
  uses: codecov/codecov-action@v4
  with:
    files: lcov.info
    fail_ci_if_error: false  # Don't fail build if upload fails
    verbose: true
```

### Coverage Configuration
Create `codecov.yml` in repo root:

```yaml
coverage:
  status:
    project:
      default:
        target: 70%
        threshold: 2%
    patch:
      default:
        target: 80%  # New code should be well-tested

comment:
  layout: "reach,diff,flags,tree"
  behavior: default
  require_changes: true

ignore:
  - "tests/**"
  - "benches/**"
  - "fuzz/**"
```

## Implementation Tasks

1. **Add Codecov upload to CI** - Modify coverage job
2. **Create codecov.yml config** - Define coverage targets and behavior
3. **Add coverage badge to README** - Show current status
4. **Verify PR comments work** - Test on a real PR

## Success Criteria

- [ ] Coverage uploads to Codecov on every push/PR
- [ ] PR comments show coverage diff
- [ ] README displays coverage badge
- [ ] Codecov dashboard shows historical trends
- [ ] No CI failures from Codecov (fail_ci_if_error: false)

## Considerations

- **Token**: Not required for public repos, but can add CODECOV_TOKEN secret for reliability
- **Coverage format**: Using LCOV (lcov.info) which Codecov supports natively
- **Existing thresholds**: Keep our enforced thresholds in CI; Codecov is additive visibility
- **Privacy**: Coverage data is public for public repos - this is fine for DCG

## References

- Codecov GitHub Action: https://github.com/codecov/codecov-action
- CASS implementation: /data/projects/coding_agent_session_search/.github/workflows/coverage.yml","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","assignee":"","created_at":"2026-01-09T19:20:04.545615215Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-11T15:17:08.430047700Z","closed_at":"2026-01-11T15:17:08.430047700Z","close_reason":"All 4 prerequisites complete: codecov.yml config, CI upload step, e2e verification, and README badge. -{"id":"git_safety_guard-6avr","title":"[E8-T2] Add git_awareness config section","description":"## Task: Add git_awareness config section

### Parent Epic
Epic 8: Git Branch-Aware Strictness (git_safety_guard-1d6e)

### What To Do
Add configuration section for git branch awareness.

### Configuration Schema
```toml
[git_awareness]
# Enable/disable git awareness
enabled = true

# Branches to treat as protected (glob patterns)
protected_branches = ["main", "master", "production", "release/*"]

# Strictness level for protected branches
# "critical" = only block critical severity
# "high" = block critical + high (more restrictive)
# "all" = block everything
protected_strictness = "all"

# Branches where rules are relaxed (glob patterns)
relaxed_branches = ["feature/*", "experiment/*", "sandbox/*"]

# Packs to disable on relaxed branches
relaxed_disabled_packs = []

# Show branch context in output
show_branch_in_output = true
```

### Rust Types
```rust
#[derive(Deserialize, Default)]
struct GitAwarenessConfig {
    enabled: bool,
    protected_branches: Vec<String>,
    protected_strictness: Strictness,
    relaxed_branches: Vec<String>,
    relaxed_disabled_packs: Vec<String>,
    show_branch_in_output: bool,
}

#[derive(Deserialize, Default)]
enum Strictness {
    Critical,  // Only critical severity
    High,      // Critical + high
    #[default]
    All,       // All severities
}
```

### Files to Modify
- src/config.rs (add GitAwarenessConfig)

Sanitizer should mask those args so destructive patterns don't match.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"","created_at":"2026-01-12T20:40:05.931971010Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-12T20:42:25.452063019Z","closed_at":"2026-01-12T20:42:25.452063019Z","close_reason":"Completed","compaction_level":0}
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-6g73","title":"[E1-T5] Implement JSON output mode","description":"## Task: Implement JSON output mode

### Parent Epic
Epic 1: Pattern Testing Tool (git_safety_guard-nimk)

### What To Do
Implement --json flag that outputs evaluation results as structured JSON for CI/CD integration and scripting.

### Implementation Details
1. JSON schema:
```json
{
  "command": "rm -rf /",
  "outcome": "blocked",
  "pattern": {
    "id": "filesystem.recursive_delete",
    "pack": "core",
    "severity": "critical",
    "regex": "rm\\s+(-[rf]+\\s+)*/"
  },
  "confidence": 0.95,
  "allowlist_checked": true,
  "allowlist_match": null,
  "evaluation_time_ms": 2
}
```

2. For allowed commands:
```json
{
  "command": "npm run build",
  "outcome": "allowed",
  "reason": "allowlist_match",
  "allowlist_rule": "npm run *"
}
```

3. Use serde_json for serialization
4. Ensure stable schema (document for users)

### Files to Modify
- src/output/test.rs (or wherever test output lives)
- src/cli.rs (handle --json flag)

### Dependencies
- E1-T2 (EvaluationResult must be Serialize)
- E1-T4 (output module must exist)","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-18T19:05:32.528072436Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-18T20:37:26.255865955Z","compaction_level":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-6g73","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-0d7g","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-18T19:15:33.944568942Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-6g73","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-p8e0","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-18T19:15:34.013287162Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","metadata":"","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-6m5","title":"[PACK] apigateway.aws - AWS API Gateway Pack","description":"# AWS API Gateway Pack

## Scope
Implement pattern matching for AWS API Gateway operations.

## Destructive Patterns
### REST APIs (APIGateway)
- `aws apigateway delete-rest-api`
- `aws apigateway delete-resource`
- `aws apigateway delete-method`
- `aws apigateway delete-stage`
- `aws apigateway delete-deployment`
- `aws apigateway delete-api-key`

### HTTP APIs (APIGatewayV2)
- `aws apigatewayv2 delete-api`
- `aws apigatewayv2 delete-route`
- `aws apigatewayv2 delete-integration`
- `aws apigatewayv2 delete-stage`

## Safe Patterns
- `aws apigateway get-rest-api`
- `aws apigateway get-resources`
- `aws apigatewayv2 get-apis`
- All list/get operations

## Implementation Notes
- Parent category: apigateway.* (git_safety_guard-5qa)
- Keywords: aws, apigateway, apigatewayv2
- Note: Both REST and HTTP API types

## Testing Requirements
- [ ] Unit tests for REST API deletion
- [ ] Unit tests for HTTP API deletion
- [ ] Unit tests for resource/route deletion
- [ ] E2E test: `scripts/e2e_tests/apigateway_aws.txt`

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] API deletion blocked
- [ ] Resource deletion blocked
- [ ] Stage deletion blocked
- [ ] Query operations allowed","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"","created_at":"2026-01-09T20:11:39.643232440Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-11T08:33:14.724055156Z","closed_at":"2026-01-11T08:33:14.724055156Z","close_reason":"Implemented AWS API Gateway pack with 18 destructive patterns, 27 safe patterns, and 15 tests","compaction_level":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-6m5","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-6ozg","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T22:47:13.041762913Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-6m5","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-ct5s","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T22:07:43.083515632Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-6m5","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-e7j","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T22:07:43.173290886Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-6m5","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-i79","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T22:07:43.039670367Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-6m5","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-ikf2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T22:07:42.990360009Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-6m5","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-jdb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T22:07:43.128910904Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-6m5","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-l968","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T22:07:43.217773160Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-6m5","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-qxc7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T22:47:12.995945099Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"","thread_id":""}]}
